title: Scorzonera alpigena
taxonomy: Scorzonera alpigena is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Scorzonera, which contains several species of knavels or scorzoneras. Scorzonera alpigena is native to the mountainous regions of central and southern Europe. The exact classification of Scorzonera alpigena may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered a part of the plant kingdom. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Scorzonera alpigena shares characteristics with other daisy-like plants.

intro: Scorzonera alpigena, also known as Alpine scorzonera or mountain salsify, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Scorzonera alpigena include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Scorzonera alpigena is commonly prepared as a root tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used to make extracts and capsules.
